REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS OF DEFENDANTS MICHAEL MAYER AND STEPHEN PERLMAN IS GRANTED, IN PART. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANTS ARE AWARDED $51,794.25 IN ATTORNEY'S FEES IN CONNECTION WITH THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. THE REQUESTED FEE AWARD WAS REDUCED BY $59.50 BECAUSE TWO ATTORNEYS BILLED TO REVIEW THE SAME TENTATIVE RULING. MR. BROWN'S FEES WERE ALSO EXCLUDED. COUNSEL FAILED TO PROVIDE AUTHORITY THAT FEES INCURRED BY A LEGAL ASSISTANT ARE RECOVERABLE. WITH RESPECT TO THE FEES INCURRED IN CONNECTION WITH THE ATTORNEY FEE MOTION, THE COURT FINDS THAT 12.5 HOURS IS UNREASONABLE. THE COURT WILL CREDIT COUNSEL WITH HALF, 5 HOURS FOR MS. CAMPBELL'S TIME AND 1 HOUR FOR MR. RIGGS TIME, FOR A TOTAL AWARD OF $55,664.25 FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. WITH RESPECT TO COSTS. DEFENDANTS ARE AWARDED $1,036.90. THE COURT CANNOT DISCERN WHAT FILING FEES DEFENDANTS INCURRED ON 11/25/24 FOR $19.36 AND ON 1/14/25 FOR $21.42. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Of Defendants Michael Mayer And Stephen Perlman
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT STEPHEN PERLMAN , AN INDIVIDUAL, MICHAEL MAYER Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs Of Defendants Michael Mayer And Stephen Perlman is GRANTED, in part. No opposition filed. Defendants are awarded $51,794.25 in attorney's fees in connection with the anti-slapp motion. The requested fee award was reduced by $59.50 because two attorneys billed to review the same tentative ruling. Mr. Brown's fees were also excluded. Counsel failed to provide authority that fees incurred by a legal assistant are recoverable. With respect to the fees incurred in connection with the attorney fee motion, the court finds that 12.5 hours is unreasonable. The court will credit counsel with half, 5 hours for Ms. Campbell's time and 1 hour for Mr. Riggs time, for a total award of $55,664.25 for attorney's fees.
With respect to costs. Defendants are awarded $1,036.90. The Court cannot discern what filing fees Defendants incurred on 11/25/24 for $19.36 and on 1/14/25 for $21.42.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion To Strike Third Cause Of Action For Aiding And Abetting A Breach Of Fiduciary Duty As A Strategic Lawsuit Against Public Participation
Off Calendar Jan-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS MICHAEL MAYER AND STEPHEN PERLMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR AIDING AND ABETTING A BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY AS A STRATEGIC LAWSUIT AGAINST PUBLIC PARTICIPATION

Appearances: Robert R. Riggs of Katzoff & Riggs LLP (707.277.9200) for defendants and moving parties; Michael G. Kim of Michael G. Kim, APC (760.579.7390) appearing via Zoom for plaintiff and opposing party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Movants established that Plaintiff's claim arose from activity protected by CCP sec. 425.16 and shifted the burden to Plaintiff. Plaintiff did not meet its burden to establish the merit of its claim by showing a probability of success. Movants' Request for Judicial Notice, pursuant to Cal. Evid. Code Sec. 452(c) and (h), is GRANTED. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice, pursuant to Cal. Evid. Code Sec. 452(d) is GRANTED. Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Jennifer Roux, CSR 11033 (T: 707.477.9599, E: jenrouxcsr@gmail.com) (501/CFH)

Robert Riggs Esq., Katzoff & Riggs, for defendant

See reporter's transcript for full details

Mr. Kim contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF 690 MARKET CLUB OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT MUTUAL BENEFIT COMMON INTEREST DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION MOTION / NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION FOR A TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE GRANTED is DENIED.
